message:
feat(sqlite): add async SQLite wrapper with blocking pool integration

Introduce optional SQLite support via rusqlite with integration into
the spawn_blocking pool for async database operations.

Changes:
- Cargo.toml: Add optional 'sqlite' feature with rusqlite bundled
  for maximum portability across platforms
- src/lib.rs: Conditionally export database module when feature enabled
- src/database/: New async wrapper module providing:
  - Connection pooling with automatic blocking thread management
  - Query execution with cancellation support
  - Transaction helpers with scope-based rollback
  - Prepared statement caching

The wrapper integrates with Asupersync's structured concurrency model,
ensuring database operations respect parent scope cancellation and
cooperate with the three-lane scheduler's blocking task handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+//! Database clients with async wrappers and Cx integration.
+//!
+//! This module provides async wrappers for database clients, integrating with
+//! asupersync's cancel-correct semantics and blocking pool.
+//!
+//! # Available Clients
+//!
+//! - [`sqlite`]: SQLite async wrapper using blocking pool
+//!
+//! # Design Philosophy
+//!
+//! Database clients use the blocking pool to execute synchronous operations
+//! without blocking the async runtime. All operations integrate with [`Cx`]
+//! for checkpointing and cancellation.
+//!
+//! [`Cx`]: crate::cx::Cx
+
+pub mod sqlite;
+
+pub use sqlite::{
+    SqliteConnection, SqliteError, SqliteRow, SqliteTransaction, SqliteValue,
+};
